rulings.Case Title: LEGAL RECOVERY, LLC VS. MARTIN LEE ENG
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice And Application Of Martin Lee Eng In Requesting An Automatic Homestead Exemption Of Lombard Property, San Francisco, California; Declaration Of Reshma Kama
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for February 29, 2024 line 1. DEFENDANT MARTIN ENG'S NOTICE AND APPLICATION OF MARTIN LEE ENG IN REQUESTING AN AUTOMATIC HOMESTEAD EXEMPTION OF LOMBARD PROPERTY, SAN FRANCISCO, CALIFORNIA is DENIED. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: SUZANNE SCHORNACK VS. PERRY BOUSSINA ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion Under Code Of Civil Procedure Section 425.16 C1 For Reasonable Attorneys Fees Incurred In Bringing A Successful Anti Slapp Motion
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for February 29, 2024 line 5. DEFENDANTS BRETT GIBBS AND NILS ROSENQUEST'S MOTION UNDER CODE OF CIVIL PROCEDURE SECTION 425.16 C1 FOR REASONABLE ATTORNEYS FEES INCURRED IN BRINGING A SUCCESSFUL ANTI SLAPP MOTION is continued to March 15, 2024. No later than March 8, 2024 Plaintiff to file a supplemental brief (not to exceed 3 pages) addressing the sufficiency of taping the motion to a door (page 3 of the proof of service on file) for an assertion of personal service. Alternatively, Plaintiff may file a supplemental declaration of the process server stating that the server handed the papers to the Defendant or to another person at defendant's residence not younger than 18 years of age. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849).
